topTargets.name: Malachi Fields
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 87.2 Starter grade with Nico Collins comp, almost certainly available at 1.08 (FBG/ADP top-10 but behind the consensus elite). Addresses a clear WR need (only 3 on roster) and the size/speed profile fits a 'best WR available' pivot at the back end of round 1.

topTargets.name: KC Concepcion
topTargets.reasoning: RSP DoT 86.3 Starter with ADP 13.57 — true first-round value who often slips into the 1.08-1.10 range. Versatile flanker/slot fit and return-game upside makes him a low-bust pick for a roster that needs WR depth and has $12.6M cap room to absorb a Y1 contract.

topTargets.name: Jordyn Tyson
topTargets.reasoning: Industry darling (ADP 12.71) RSP grade at 84.5. With medium RSP affinity, MWS will weight the consensus heavily — if Tyson is on the board at 1.08 the boom-bust ceiling is hard to pass on for a team trying to find a long-term WR1.

topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: RSP 84.6 Rotational Starter, undervalued by ADP. With back-to-back 2.02/2.07 and four picks in round 2, MWS can grab a bell-cow profile here without overpaying. Six expiring contracts mean cheap rookie deals matter.

topTargets.name: Eli Stowers
topTargets.reasoning: RSP 84.4 with Starter-tier upside as a former QB-turned-move-TE. Already 3 TEs but only one rookie-cost asset; at 2.07/2.08 or 2.14, Stowers is an RSP-favored value pick that fits the 'blend RSP with consensus' style.

capPosture: Healthy at $32.4M used / $12.6M space; can active-roster two rookie deals comfortably, but with 10 picks in R1-3 and only 18 roster spots, several Day-3 selections will go to taxi or be stashed.

wildcard.name: Skyler Bell
wildcard.reasoning: RSP DoT 85.9 with a Ja'Marr Chase high-end comp but no ADP presence — pure RSP-affinity dart. With four R2 picks (2.02, 2.07, 2.08, 2.14), MWS can absorb a contrarian swing here while still hitting consensus value elsewhere.

summary: Midwestside Connection is sitting on 10 picks in the top 3 rounds with $12.6M cap space and 6 expiring contracts — this is a reload draft. Expect a WR-heavy approach starting with the best receiver available at 1.08 (Fields/Concepcion/Tyson tier), a round-2 RB to refresh the backfield, and several lottery-ticket taxi stashes in round 3. Medium RSP affinity means they'll lean consensus in R1 but reach for Waldman favorites once the volume picks start in R2-R3.
